description: Letter from Nethanel b. Ḥalfon to the Cairene notable ʿOvadya b. Benayahu. He asks for help for a learned man from Europe who has traveled to Cairo solely because he had heard of the generosity of the addressee's father. The request is urgent since the foreigner wishes to be on the high seas by Passover. On the verso, a similar letter by the foreigner himself to another notable, Maymun, in the Egyptian capital. Nethanel's letter obviously was returned to the foreigner by the addressee and was then used by him for a draft which, after having been copied, was thrown into the Geniza. Dated ca. 1170's. (Information from Mediterranean Society, II, p. 499)
